title: Mask value to N bits using shr(256-N, shl(256-N, value)) — no mask constant needed
trigger_patterns: truncate value to specific bit width
clean dirty upper bits of smaller uint
description: shl(256-N, value) shifts the value left so only the lowest N bits survive (upper bits are lost). shr(256-N, result) shifts back to position 0. Result is value masked to exactly N bits. No mask constant like 0xffffffffffff needed — the shift pair acts as the mask.
pattern_before:
// Mask constant approach
uint96 cleaned = feeNumerator & type(uint96).max;
when_to_apply: Cleaning dirty upper bits of any value that should fit in N bits, especially function parameters that may have dirty bits from ABI decoding
when_not_to_apply: When the value is already known clean from prior operations
